question: An input mapping calls FEEL `date()` on a string supplied by an upstream system. What does the expression shown evaluate to?

options.text: `null` — `"2024-06-31"` is not a valid calendar date because June has only 30 days.
`date("2024-07-01")` — the engine rolls the invalid day over to the next month.
`date("2024-06-30")` — the engine clamps the day to the last valid day of the month.
An incident is raised at the activity because the date is malformed.

a.text: `date(from: string)` parses the string into a date and returns `null` if the string is not a valid calendar date. The docs explicitly use `"2024-06-31"` as the example of an invalid date because June only has 30 days.

b.text: FEEL `date()` does not silently roll an invalid day forward into the next month; it returns `null`.

c.text: FEEL `date()` does not clamp the day to the last valid day of the month; an invalid calendar date simply returns `null`.

d.text: Returning `null` from `date()` is not an incident on its own. An incident would follow only if a downstream consumer required a non-null date.

explanation: `date(from)` parses a string in ISO date format and returns the corresponding date. When the string is syntactically well-formed but is not a valid calendar date (such as June 31), the function returns `null` rather than rolling, clamping, or raising an error.
